perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will endeavor not to Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will Compete' to 'will endeavor not to Compete' introduces ambiguity, weakening the obligation. 'Endeavor' implies an attempt, but not a guarantee of non-competition.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio law regarding non-compete agreements requires that they are reasonable and necessary to protect the employer's legitimate business interests. By using 'endeavor,' the agreement becomes less enforceable and thus potentially unreasonable as it doesn't provide a clear commitment from the Shareholder not to compete, undermining the protection the law seeks to provide the Company.
perturbation.location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE, b) IN GENERAL.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Section 1331.04 - Ohio Revised Code | Ohio Laws Skip to main content Back To Top Top The Legislative Service Commission staff updates the Revised Code on an ongoing basis, as it completes its act review of enacted legislation. Updates may be slower during some times of the year, depending on the volume of enacted legislation. Section 1331.04 | Conspiracy against trade prohibited. Ohio Revised Code / Title 13 Commercial Transactions / Chapter 1331 Monopolies Effective: April 6, 2017 Latest Legislation: Senate Bill 227 - 131st General Assembly PDF: Download Authenticated PDF Every combination, contract, or agreement in the form of a trust is declared to be a conspiracy against trade and illegal. No person shall engage in such conspiracy or take part therein, or aid or advise in its commission, or, as principal, manager, director, agent, servant, or employer, or in any other capacity, knowingly carry out any of the stipulations, purposes, prices, or rates, or furnish any information to assist in carrying out such purposes, or orders thereunder, or in pursuance thereof, or in any manner violate sections 1331.01 to 1331.14 of the Revised Code. Each day's violation of this section is a separate offense. Available Versions of this Section October 1, 1953 – House Bill 1 - 100th General Assembly [ View October 1, 1953 Version ] April 6, 2017 – Senate Bill 227 - 131st General Assembly [ View April 6, 2017 Version ]
perturbation.justification: The modification from "will Compete" to "will endeavor not to Compete" introduces ambiguity that weakens the enforceability of the non-compete agreement. The law explanation states that Ohio law requires non-compete agreements to be reasonable and necessary to protect the employer's legitimate business interests. By using 'endeavor,' the agreement becomes less enforceable, potentially undermining the protection the law seeks to provide. The original text provided a clear commitment not to compete, whereas the modified text only commits to attempting not to compete, creating an ambiguous legal obligation. The scraped law snippet 1 discusses conspiracy against trade, and while it doesn't directly address non-compete enforceability, it suggests a concern about agreements that restrain trade, which reinforces the importance of clear and enforceable terms in a non-compete agreement.

perturbation.original_text: From the date hereof, Shareholder will maintain in strict confidence and will not, directly or indirectly, or through Shareholder's Affiliates, divulge, transmit, publish, release, or otherwise use of cause to be used in any manner contrary to the interests of the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, any confidential information relating to the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates' systems, operations, products, services, business methods, management practices, contracts, computer programs and data bases, records, development data and reports, quality control specifications, cost analyses, flow charts, know-how, consumer lists, supplier lists, prospects, market development programs, acquisition programs, personnel data, or any information relating to customers, suppliers, products, sales, acquisitions, acquisition plans, financial structure, or pricing, and other information of like nature.
perturbation.changed_text: From the date hereof, Shareholder will consider maintaining in confidence and will attempt not to, directly or indirectly, or through Shareholder's Affiliates, divulge, transmit, publish, release, or otherwise use or cause to be used in any manner contrary to the interests of the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, any confidential information relating to the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates' systems, operations, products, services, business methods, management practices, contracts, computer programs and data bases, records, development data and reports, quality control specifications, cost analyses, flow charts, know-how, consumer lists, supplier lists, prospects, market development programs, acquisition programs, personnel data, or any information relating to customers, suppliers, products, sales, acquisitions, acquisition plans, financial structure, or pricing, and other information of like nature.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will maintain in strict confidence' to 'will consider maintaining in confidence' and 'will not' to 'will attempt not to' introduces significant ambiguity. This weakens the confidentiality obligation, making it discretionary rather than mandatory.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio law protects trade secrets, requiring reasonable efforts to maintain their secrecy. The modified language weakens the commitment to confidentiality, which may be insufficient to meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard required to protect trade secrets under Ohio law.
perturbation.location: SECTION 2. - CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Ohio Revised Code | Ohio Laws Skip to main content Back To Top Top Number Not Found No Ohio Revised Code section number corresponds to "1333.51".
perturbation.justification: The original contract requires the shareholder to 'maintain in strict confidence' and 'will not' disclose confidential information. The modified text weakens this obligation by changing it to 'consider maintaining in confidence' and 'will attempt not to', introducing ambiguity about the shareholder's commitment to confidentiality. The Law Explanation states this weakened commitment may be insufficient to meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard required to protect trade secrets under Ohio law. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not found and Snippet 2 results in a 404, the provided Law Explanation directly states that Ohio law protects trade secrets, requiring reasonable efforts. This weakening of the obligation could lead to a breach of the duty to protect trade secrets under Ohio law, creating a contradiction as the modified text introduces ambiguity and discretion where a strict obligation existed.

perturbation.original_text: The Restricted Area is deemed to include, at a minimum, the States of Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky and Wisconsin.
perturbation.changed_text: The Restricted Area is generally understood to include, but is not limited to, the States of Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky and Wisconsin.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'is deemed to include, at a minimum' with 'is generally understood to include, but is not limited to' weakens the geographical scope of the non-compete agreement. The use of 'generally understood' and 'not limited to' creates uncertainty about the actual restricted area.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio law requires that non-compete agreements be reasonable in terms of geographical scope. By making the restricted area ambiguous, the agreement becomes potentially unreasonable and unenforceable because the employee cannot clearly understand the boundaries of the restriction. A specific geographic scope strengthens the enforceability of a non-compete agreement in Ohio.
perturbation.location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE, a) DEFINITIONS. iii)

perturbation.justification: The change from 'is deemed to include, at a minimum' to 'is generally understood to include, but is not limited to' introduces ambiguity regarding the geographic scope of the non-compete agreement. The Law Explanation states that Ohio law requires non-compete agreements to be reasonable in geographical scope, and that an ambiguous area could render the agreement unenforceable. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 pertains to conspiracy against trade, the general legal principle that contracts must be clear and definite to be enforceable is widely accepted. The Perturbation Explanation confirms the introduction of uncertainty. Therefore, the modified text creates a legal contradiction by potentially undermining the enforceability of the non-compete agreement due to its ambiguous geographic scope.

perturbation.original_text: Upon demand by the Company, and in any event upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon reasonable demand by the Company, and hopefully upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will consider delivering to the Company relevant original and facsimile records, documents and data, if readily available, in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing 'reasonable demand,' 'hopefully upon termination,' 'consider delivering,' and 'if readily available' significantly weakens the obligation to return company records. This makes compliance discretionary and dependent on subjective factors.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio law on trade secrets requires reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy. By weakening the obligation to return company records (which often contain trade secrets), the agreement fails to provide sufficient protection for the company's confidential information and trade secrets. The return of documents upon termination is a reasonable measure and making it discretionary weakens that protection.
perturbation.location: SECTION 2. - CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Ohio Revised Code | Ohio Laws Skip to main content Back To Top Top Number Not Found No Ohio Revised Code section number corresponds to "1333.51".
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the shareholder to deliver all company records upon termination of services. The changed text weakens this obligation by introducing 'reasonable demand,' 'hopefully upon termination,' 'consider delivering,' and 'if readily available.' While Ohio Revised Code section number does not correspond to "1333.51", as indicated by the scraped snippet, the LLM explanation states that Ohio law requires reasonable efforts to maintain trade secret secrecy. The modified text, making the return of documents discretionary, contradicts this requirement, thereby failing to provide sufficient protection for the company's confidential information.
